channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Man Arrested After Home Invasion Incident Involving Nursing Students
item.description: A 34-year-old man has been taken into police custody following a home invasion incident in Perth's west. The alleged offender, dressed only in a cap, allegedly breached the residence of three young nu...

item.title: Ahsan Iqbal Discusses Institutional Harmony and Devolution of Power in Pakistan
item.description: Federal Minister Ahsan Iqbal emphasized that Pakistan lacks a hybrid system, with all state institutions aligned for national interests. During his Houston visit, he highlighted institutional harmony ...

item.title: Rivers Governor Unveils N1.85 Trillion 2026 Budget Plan
item.description: Rivers State Governor Siminalayi Fubara has unveiled a proposed budget of N1.854 trillion for the 2026 fiscal year, describing it as a 'Budget of Resilience for Growth and Development.' The plan aims ...

item.title: Gauteng Police Target Illegal Mining Networks in West Rand Operation
item.description: Over 70 suspected illegal miners were arrested during a coordinated operation in Mohlakeng, Gauteng, as part of efforts to dismantle illegal mining networks. The operation, codenamed Prosper, saw poli...

item.title: DMW to Seek Reconsideration of Ombudsman's Dismissal in P1.4B Land Deal Case
item.description: The Department of Migrant Workers (DMW) plans to file a motion for reconsideration with the Office of the Ombudsman regarding its dismissal of graft and plunder charges against former Overseas Workers...

item.title: Pauline Hanson's Support Wanes as Monocultural Festival Proposed
item.description: The article discusses the declining support for Pauline Hanson and her One Nation party, with a focus on the proposed monocultural festival as a response to perceived multiculturalism challenges. The ...

item.title: Tamil Nadu CM Vijay Visits Karur Post-Stampede, Announces Relief Measures
item.description: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay made his first visit to Karur district a year after a stampede during a rally that killed 41 people. During the visit, he criticized the DMK for politicizing t...

item.title: Train Hits School Bus With Over 70 Students, Teacher Dies in Uganda Accident
item.description: A passenger train collided with a school bus carrying more than 70 high school students from Mwebaza High School on a study trip in Mukono, Uganda, on July 10. The accident occurred at the Namumira ra...

item.title: Islamic scholars issue fatwa banning cryptocurrency transactions
item.description: Renowned Islamic scholar Mufti Taqi Usmani, along with five other scholars, has issued a fatwa declaring purchases using cryptocurrency impermissible under Islamic law. The ruling, issued by Darul Ift...

item.title: CRA delays and account lockouts plague Canadian taxpayers
item.description: Alex Pilon, a resident of Thunder Bay, Ont., faced significant issues after returning a mistaken $24,274 tax refund to the Canada Revenue Agency (CRA). Despite contacting the CRA and repaying the full...

item.title: Journalism Ethics: Importance of Fact-Checking in Reporting
item.description: The article discusses the importance of verifying information before publishing news. It highlights how journalists often fall into the trap of reporting unverified claims without proper context. For ...

item.title: Global Study Links Heatwaves to Increased Health Risks and Calls for Climate Adaptation Strategies
item.description: A comprehensive global study published in Nature Health has uncovered the significant health risks associated with heatwaves, emphasizing their growing threat due to climate change. The research, led ...

item.title: BARMM Nutrition Commission Launches to Combat Malnutrition
item.description: The Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao (BARMM) has officially launched its Nutrition Commission, marking a significant step in addressing malnutrition and improving public health. The com...

item.title: EU warns Meta to alter Facebook and Instagram's design to protect users
item.description: The European Union has warned Meta to change the 'addictive design' of Facebook and Instagram or face heavy fines. The EU accused Meta of failing to protect users, especially children and vulnerable a...

item.title: The Need for Civil Religion in America's Political Discourse
item.description: The article argues that America is neither a Christian nation nor a secular one, emphasizing the necessity of a revival of civil religion to foster national unity. The author critiques the divisive fo...

item.title: Black Leaders Advocate for Redistricting Reform in Democratic Party
item.description: The article discusses the growing influence of Black leaders within the Democratic Party who are advocating for significant changes in the redistricting process. These leaders argue that current redis...

item.title: Police Officer Accused of Pushing Lover from 6th Floor Balcony, Leaving Her Paralyzed
item.description: A Kenyan police officer, Agnes Mudigani Ndanu, is at the center of a disturbing domestic violence case after her girlfriend, Nicole Akinyi, was allegedly pushed from a sixth-floor balcony in Kasarani,...

item.title: Kevin James explores maturity and heartbreak in 'Solo Mio'
item.description: The article reviews Kevin James' latest film, Solo Mio, which follows his character Matt Taylor, an art teacher navigating heartbreak after a wedding cancellation. The movie blends romantic comedy tro...

item.title: Surgeons Use Humanoid Robots to Perform First Minimally Invasive Surgeries on Live Pigs
item.description: In a groundbreaking medical experiment, humanoid robots controlled by skilled surgeons successfully removed gallbladders from live pigs during a preclinical trial published in Nature. The teleoperated...

item.title: Tanzania expands rural connectivity with 758 new telecom towers
item.description: Tanzania has been recognized internationally for its telecommunications infrastructure project, which deployed 758 new communication towers to improve connectivity in rural areas. The initiative, laun...
